title: ClassChangeRecipe
description:
A SEPAL Class Change recipe. Compares two categorical (classified) images of the same area, encodes per-pixel class transitions, and — when both inputs carry per-class probability bands — emits a confidence band measuring how reliable each transition is.

**What this recipe is for.** Detect land-cover transitions between two classification snapshots: which pixels went from forest to non-forest, from cropland to urban, etc. The output is a single integer per pixel encoding the (from-class, to-class) pair.

**Pipeline.** (1) Load `fromImage` and `toImage` (each is a SEPAL classification recipe or an EE asset). (2) Look up each pixel's class in the `from` image and the `to` image. (3) Encode the pair as an integer `transition = fromIndex * len(toValues) + toIndex + 1` (1-based; `fromIndex`/`toIndex` are positions in each image's sorted legend values). (4) When both inputs have `probability_<value>` bands AND identical legend entry sets, compute a `confidence` band combining the per-pixel certainty of both classifications; pixels below `options.minConfidence` are reassigned to whichever side's class is most probable.

**AOI semantics.** No separate AOI field — the AOI is the intersection of the geometries of `fromImage` and `toImage`.

**Legend handling.** Unlike most recipes, `legend.entries` here is **auto-generated by the GUI** from the cartesian product of `fromImage.legendEntries` × `toImage.legendEntries`. The user can edit labels and colors but cannot change the number or value of entries. The LLM should leave `legend` absent when constructing a recipe; the GUI populates it on first edit. The EE backend does not read `legend` — it derives the transition encoding directly from each input image's `legendEntries`.

This schema describes the persisted `model` object of an *initialized* recipe. Cross-field constraints that JSON Schema cannot express are enforced by the companion rules.

dates.description: Optional cosmetic record of the from/to dates the user is comparing. The EE backend does NOT read this field — date semantics are entirely captured by the underlying input classifications. Included for round-trip compatibility with the GUI's default model.

inputImage.title: ClassChangeInputImage
inputImage.description: A single classified source image. The classification can come from another SEPAL recipe (typically a `CLASSIFICATION` recipe) or directly from an Earth Engine Image / ImageCollection asset whose bands include per-class probabilities.

type.description: What `id` refers to. `RECIPE_REF` — id of another SEPAL recipe whose image output is consumed (typically a `CLASSIFICATION` recipe). `ASSET` — Earth Engine Image / ImageCollection asset id.

id.description: SEPAL recipe id (when type=RECIPE_REF) or Earth Engine asset id (when type=ASSET).

band.description: Single band of the source image to use as the class label. Typically `'class'` for SEPAL classification recipes.

bands.description: Per-band visualization metadata, keyed by band name. Each entry holds `values`, `labels`, and `palette` arrays mirroring the source's categorical visualization. Populated by the GUI when the image is selected; used by the EE backend to detect probability bands (`probability_<value>`) for the optional confidence calculation. The LLM does not need to synthesize this; pull it from the loaded recipe/asset state.

legendEntries.description: The legend of the source classification — `(value, label, color)` per class. The GUI restricts this to at most 10 entries (a hard MAX_LEGEND_ENTRIES = 10 in the input panel) because the cartesian product `len(from) × len(to)` would otherwise produce too many transition classes to label meaningfully. Mirrors the source classification's `legend.entries`.

metadata.description: Optional Earth Engine asset metadata (band specs, properties, etc.). Cosmetic / cached-from-load. The LLM should leave it absent.

visualizations.description: Optional visualization presets cached from the source. Cosmetic; the LLM should leave it absent.

legend.title: ClassChangeLegend
legend.description: Display legend for the `transition` band. **Auto-generated by the GUI** from the cartesian product of `fromImage.legendEntries × toImage.legendEntries`. The LLM should not author this from scratch when creating a recipe — leaving it absent lets the GUI synthesize it on first edit. The EE backend does not read `legend.entries`; the transition encoding is derived directly from each input's `legendEntries`.

entries.description: One entry per `(fromClass, toClass)` pair. Values are 1-based and follow the encoding `fromIndex * len(toValues) + toIndex + 1`. Labels are `"<fromLabel> -> <toLabel>"`.

id.description: Optional UUID for stable React keys.

value.description: Class value (must be unique across legend entries).

color.description: Hex color (`#RRGGBB`) used when visualizing this class.

options.title: ClassChangeOptions
options.description: Optional confidence-thresholding behavior. Only meaningful when both `fromImage` AND `toImage` carry per-class probability bands (`probability_<value>`) AND their legend entry sets are identical — otherwise the recipe does not produce a `confidence` band and these options are ignored.

minConfidence.description:
Minimum combined per-pixel confidence (`mean(toCertainty, fromCertainty)` where each certainty is the probability of the assigned class minus the probability of the other side's class) for a transition to be considered reliable. Pixels below this threshold are reassigned to whichever side's class is more probable, suppressing low-confidence transitions.

Default `0` (no thresholding). The GUI slider exposes 0-100 with ticks at 0, 10, 25, 50, 75, 90, 100. The slider is disabled when probability bands are not available on both inputs.
